medical_history.chief_complaint: Lower abdominal discomfort that radiated to the left knee
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Developmental delay noted at 12 months, began walking at 24 months, diagnosed with Legg-Calvé-Perthes disease at 3 years and 11 months
medical_history.past_medical_history: Albright hereditary osteodystrophy, hypothyroidism, hypoprolactinemia, anemia
medical_history.family_history: Unremarkable for any genetic conditions, no parental consanguinity

physical_examination.general_examination: Dysmorphic features (large tongue, low-set ears, deeply tethered skin creases in upper limbs, blue-to-pink collagenous subcutaneous lesions on abdomen), plagiocephaly, round face with midface hypoplasia, underdeveloped ears, abnormally spaced small teeth, short stature, protuberant abdomen
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Unusually shaped hands and feet with thickened small toenails, short fingers with prominent pads, bilateral potter's thumb, clinodactyly of small finger bilaterally, limited hip abduction to 45°

laboratory_examinations.findings: Negative for Prader-Willi/Angelman syndrome, interpreted as a normal karyotype

Marked hypocalcemia (calcium level, 1.57 mg/dL [0.39 mmol/L]) with hyperphosphatemia (phosphate level, 2.77 mg/dL [0.89 mmol/L]). Circulating parathyroid hormone level was markedly elevated (194 pg/mL [194 ng/L])
Presence of the 187del4 mutation in exon 7 of the GNAS1 gene—the genotype of Albright hereditary osteodystrophy
Consistent with anemia, hypothyroidism, and hypoprolactinemia

radiographic_examinations.date: At three years and eleven months of age
radiographic_examinations.findings: Demonstrated Legg-Calvé-Perthes disease of the left hip joint

radiographic_examinations.date: At four years and four months of age
radiographic_examinations.findings: Flattening and fragmentation of the left femoral capital epiphysis with widening of the physis consistent with a diagnosis of Legg-Calvé-Perthes disease. New bone growth within the lateral aspect of the epiphysis indicated healing
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: Compared with the abdominal radiographs made at three years and eleven months of age

radiographic_examinations.date: At four years and four months of age
radiographic_examinations.findings: Indicated a bone age of approximately five years and nine months, which was determined to be abnormally accelerated for her chronological age
Showed the left femoral capital epiphysis to be confluent without fragmentation. There were no noticeable metaphyseal cysts
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: Compared with radiographs made at four years and four months of age

other_tests.findings: Dysmorphic features such as a large tongue and low-set ears. Deeply tethered skin creases in the upper limbs and blue-to-pink collagenous subcutaneous lesions on the abdomen

Plagiocephaly, a markedly round face with midface hypoplasia, underdeveloped ears, and abnormally spaced, small, "peg-like" teeth. Depressed nasal bridge with a short nose and a long, well-formed philtrum. The tongue was quite prominent. Unusually shaped hands and feet, with thickened, small toenails. The fingers were short with prominent pads. Bilateral potter's thumb with increased thumbnail width-to-length ratios. The fourth metacarpophalangeal joint was noted to be proximally displaced and she had clinodactyly of the small finger bilaterally

other_tests.date: At three years and eleven months of age
other_tests.findings: Painless passive range of motion, which was normal in all planes except abduction; abduction was limited to 45°

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was made based on radiographs showing sclerosis and flattening of the left femoral head with fragmentation and lateral extrusion of the capital epiphysis. The patient's symptoms included lower abdominal discomfort radiating to the left knee and limited abduction of the left hip. The prognosis was deemed favorable due to the patient's young age at diagnosis.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: to improve abduction of the left hip
